---
title: "species_number_per_biome"
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

```{r setup}
library(biomes)
library(tidyverse)
```


## Species per biome
Often it is relevant to know the number of species per biome.
You can easily use the biomes package together with basic data wrangling using the tidyverse 
package to answer this question.

For a single layer:
```{r}
class <- biomes_example %>%
  biomes_classify(biome = layers[[1]])

biomes_example %>%
  bind_cols(class) %>%
  group_by(species, biome_name) %>% 
  count()
```

For multiple biome layers:
```{r}
class <- biomes_example %>%
  biomes_classify(biome = layers[[c(1, 17)]])

biomes_example %>%
  bind_cols(class) %>%
  select(species, contains("Biome_Inventory")) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = contains("Biome_Inventory"),
               names_to = "layer",
               values_to = "value") %>%
  distinct() %>%
  group_by(layer, value) %>%
  count()
```
